title: Email Session Expired Repeatedly
description: Troubleshooting repeated email session expiry or being signed out automatically.

steps.title: Sign Out and Sign Back In
steps.instruction:
*🔄 Step 1: Sign Out and Sign Back In*
• Completely sign out of your email account.
• Close all browser tabs and windows.
• Reopen your browser and log back into your email.
• If using an email app (Outlook, Zoho), close it fully and reopen.
_Expected: A fresh session starts and session expiry issue is resolved._

steps.title: Clear Browser Cookies and Cache
steps.instruction:
*🧹 Step 2: Clear Browser Cookies and Cache*
• In your browser, press *Ctrl + Shift + Delete*.
• Select *All time* as the time range.
• Check *Cookies and other site data* and *Cached images and files*.
• Click *Clear data*, then log back into your email.
_Expected: Old/corrupt session data is cleared, preventing premature expiry._

steps.title: Check Browser Settings — Block Third-Party Cookies
steps.instruction:
*⚙️ Step 3: Check Browser Cookie Settings*
• Some browsers block cookies that email services need to keep you signed in.
• In Chrome: *Settings → Privacy and Security → Cookies → Allow all cookies*.
• In Edge: *Settings → Privacy → Balanced or Basic* mode.
• Try signing in again after adjusting settings.
_Expected: Browser allows session cookies, preventing auto sign-out._

steps.title: Update Your Email App
steps.instruction:
*⬆️ Step 4: Update Your Email App*
• If using a desktop email client (Outlook, Thunderbird, Zoho Mail app):
  - Open the app → Go to *Help → Check for Updates* (or similar).
  - Install any available updates.
• Outdated apps often have session management bugs.
_Expected: Updated app maintains session correctly._

steps.title: Check Account Security Settings
steps.instruction:
*🔐 Step 5: Check Account Security / Session Policies*
• Some accounts are configured by IT to expire sessions after a certain period (e.g., 8 hours) for security.
• Log in to your email web portal and check *Settings → Security → Active Sessions*.
• If sessions are being revoked, it could indicate suspicious activity — raise a ticket with IT.
• Provide: your email address, browser/device used, and how often sessions expire.
_Expected: IT confirms whether this is a policy setting or a security issue._
